title: HoYoverse — Wish and Gacha Disputes Standard Operating Procedure

sections.heading: Purpose & Scope
sections.content:
This procedure defines how Player Support agents handle wish (gacha) disputes and inquiries. It covers travelers who are frustrated about not obtaining a featured 5-star character, believe the pity counter is wrong, cannot find a wish in their history, or pulled on the wrong banner by mistake.

Gacha concerns are emotional calls. Every interaction must pair accurate information with genuine empathy — the traveler has invested time and often money.

sections.heading: Authentication Requirements
sections.content:
Before discussing wish details, verify the caller owns the account:
1. UID (required) — e.g., 832458917
2. HoYoverse Email on file (required) — e.g., knakamura@email.com

Do not share specific wish history, pity counters, or character drops until both factors are confirmed.

sections.heading: Step-by-Step Procedure
sections.content:
1. Greet the caller warmly. Listen fully to the complaint before speaking
2. Authenticate using UID and HoYoverse Email
3. Call get_player_account and get_active_banners to confirm which banner the caller is wishing on
4. Call get_wish_history for the last 90 days on the relevant banner
5. Call get_pity_counter to retrieve current 5-star and 4-star pity values
6. Explain the pity position plainly — translate numbers into what it means for the traveler's next pull
7. If the traveler is in soft pity (pulls 75–89 on a 5-star banner), reassure: the next 1–10 wishes are dramatically more likely to award the 5-star
8. If the traveler pulled on the wrong banner, explain the Fate type rules and check for one-time goodwill exception eligibility (see Section 6)
9. If a 5-star appears in history the traveler does not remember, check whether it was converted to Starglitter/Stardust (constellations beyond C6)
10. If compensation is requested and not warranted, explain the system once more with empathy — do not defensively cite policy
11. Offer to enable in-app wish tips and soft pity notifications
12. Thank the traveler for their patience and confirm next steps before closing

sections.heading: Tone Guidance — This Is Critical
sections.content:
Wish disputes are the single most common category of Player Support calls where tone determines outcome. Follow these principles:

- Lead with empathy: 'I completely understand the frustration — you've invested a lot of wishes.'
- Never read policy verbatim. Translate it: 'You are at pull 82, which means soft pity is already active — your chances in the next 1–10 wishes are much higher than normal.'
- Avoid phrases like 'within expected parameters' or 'according to policy'. Use 'what this means for you...' instead
- Validate the emotion even when you cannot override the mechanic: 'It is absolutely frustrating, and I get why you'd feel this way.'
- Offer agency: 'Would you like me to turn on the in-app pity tracker so you can see your counter in real time?'
- Never make the traveler feel dismissed. A call that ends with 'I understand, good luck on your next pulls' is a lost traveler.

sections.heading: Soft Pity Explanation Script
sections.content:
When a traveler is frustrated about being deep in pity without a 5-star, use this script:

'I can see you are at pull [N] on the [Banner Name] banner, and I totally understand the frustration. Good news — at pull 75 the game activates what we call "soft pity." Between pulls 75 and 90 your chance of pulling a 5-star jumps dramatically from the base 0.6 percent to well over 30 percent per wish. Statistically, the vast majority of travelers in your position get their 5-star within the next 10 wishes. Hard pity at pull 90 guarantees it, but most people never reach 90 because soft pity catches them first. You are very close, and I am rooting for a great pull.'

Pair this explanation with an offer to enable in-app notifications and, where appropriate, a small goodwill gesture like 1 Intertwined Fate for loyal travelers.

sections.heading: System Actions
sections.content:
- get_player_account: Retrieve account snapshot by UID. Always first.
- get_active_banners: Confirm which banners are currently live so the agent can distinguish Character Event, Weapon Event, and Standard wishes.
- get_wish_history: Pull the last 90 days of wishes with timestamps and results.
- get_pity_counter: Current 5-star and 4-star pity counters per banner type (Character, Weapon, Standard).
- issue_goodwill_fates: Issue 1–3 Intertwined Fates as a goodwill gesture. Requires supervisor pre-approval for issuance above 1 Fate.

sections.heading: Escalation Criteria
sections.content:
Escalate to a Wish System Specialist (ext. 7100) when:
- Traveler reports 5-star pulls not appearing in Wish History after 30 minutes (possible data sync issue)
- Traveler claims a pity reset occurred without their knowledge
- Compensation request exceeds the agent's issuance threshold (1 Fate)
- Traveler threatens to file a dispute with a payment processor or regulatory body
- Systemic issue suspected (multiple travelers in same session reporting similar symptoms)

Transfer Destinations:
- Wish System Specialist (ext. 7100): Pity anomalies, missing history entries
- Goodwill Team (ext. 7150): Compensation beyond standard 1-Fate issuance
- Retention Team (ext. 7250): Traveler threatens to quit / churn risk

sections.heading: Common Issues & Resolutions
sections.content:
Issue: Traveler at pull 80 without a 5-star, extremely frustrated
Resolution: Use the soft pity script (Section 5). Offer to enable in-app pity notifications. Consider a goodwill 1 Fate for travelers with Welkin Moon or Battle Pass Premium.

Issue: Traveler pulled on Standard Banner expecting a limited 5-star
Resolution: Explain Acquaint vs Intertwined Fate rules. If this is the traveler's first time making this mistake AND they used 10 Acquaint Fates or fewer, escalate for one-time goodwill conversion.

Issue: 5-star in history the traveler does not recognise
Resolution: Check if it was a duplicate converted at constellation C6 (auto-converts to Stardust). Explain the mechanic and show the exact conversion in wish history.

Issue: Traveler threatens to quit
Resolution: Validate emotion. Offer to stay on the line while they vent. Transfer to Retention Team (ext. 7250) if goodwill gesture alone will not resolve.

Issue: Traveler does not understand the 50/50 system
Resolution: Walk through a simple example using their own wish history. Confirm whether their next 5-star on Character Event banners is guaranteed or 50/50.
